results.title: 5 Ways to Control Google Slides from Your Phone
results.content:
### 2 Remote for Slides

Another simplest way to control Google Slides from your phone is to use the "Remote for Slides" app and "Remote for Slides" extension. This tool transforms your phone into a remote control for your Google Slides presentations.

remote for slides

Steps to use Remote for Slides Extension:

Step 1. Download the extension from the Chrome Web Browser Store. After installing it, open your presentation slides in Edit Mode, then hit the "Present w/Remote" button.

click remote present

Step 2. Next, hit the "Start Remote" button, and it will show you a 6-digit code.

nter 6 digit code on your phone

Step 3. Make sure both devices are connected to the same Wi-Fi network. [...] Step 4. Then, follow the app's prompts to link your phone to the presentation and control it remotely.

remote control google slides from phone

note

Note

Many presenters wonder if there’s a way to make their Google Slides appear in full-screen mode without showing the browser's toolbar or tabs. With Remote for Slides, you can achieve a clean presentation view, hiding unwanted elements from the screen and making your presentation look more professional.   
 This method is ideal for users who want to easily control slides from their phones without complex setups.

### 3 Convert Google Slides into Canva

Another option for remotely controlling Google Slides is converting your presentation into Canva. Canva offers a built-in feature to control slides from your phone. [...] Free Download

mirrorto-banner

# 5 Ways to Control Google Slides from Your Phone

Category:  Mirror Tips

article_time

4 mins read

Today, it is expected to do presentations online as people search for alternative ways of managing their slides without using a regular clicker. There are instances when some people have a clicker that is either broken, missing, or not around. It could also be that one would focus on their phone rather than on the computer screen. So they want to control Google Slides from a phone.

control google slides from phone

results.title: Use Your Phone as a Google Slide Presentation Remote – TCEA TechNotes Blog
results.content:
To begin controlling your presentations from your phone:

1. Open your presentation in Google Slides.
2. On the top right, click the “Present with Remote” button.
3. Wait until the presentation is fully loaded.
4. Click on the “Show ID & Start Remote” button to view the 6-digit code.
5. Open  on your phone and enter the code that appears on the page.
6. Press the Connect button and you are ready to go! Your phone is now a remote, with two large buttons to move back and forth between slides.

## Try It on Your Phone

The next time you have a presentation, try using your phone as the remote. It works like a charm. Also, don’t forget to check out Fabulous Presentations with Google Slides to learn how to create more moving and engaging presentations. [...] To install the extension:

1. Launch the Chrome browser and go to the Remote for Slides Chrome extension.
2. Click the Add to Chrome button.
3. Click Add extension in the confirmation box.
4. The button changes to Checking. Then it changes to Added to Chrome when the installation is complete.

## Using Remote for Slides

To begin controlling your presentations from your phone: [...] Google Slide presentation remote

166K

At the recent Elementary Technology Conference, I was able to use my phone as a remote to control my Google Slide presentations. Thanks to the Remote for Slides Chrome extension, I wasn’t tethered to my laptop. I was able to walk around the room with my phone and advance my slides as I presented. When presenting, why be stuck in the front of a room when you don’t have to be?

## Features of the Remote to Slides Extension

The free Remote for Slides extension (@remoteforslides) was created by Henry Lim, a Google Developer Expert in Web Technologies. Some of the features on the extension include:phone

Next, click on the “Start Remote” button to show the 6-digit code. 4. Enter the 6-digit code in the Remote for Slides Progressive Web App (s.limhenry.xyz) or in the Remote for Slides Lite Android App

results.title: Remote for Slides Chrome Extension
results.content:
your phone so if you have Safari on your phone or Chrome on your phone you can go to either either web browser and just go to remote for slides.com and there's also an Android app so if you have an Android phone I don't have an Android phone I have an iPhone but if I did I would just download the remote for slides Android app here is what it looks like on my phone I simply type in that six digit code and then click connect and now I can advance my slides just next and previous it's pretty simple I can also look at my speaker notes by clicking that little icon in the upper right hand corner so with this application on your phone you can walk around your room and present and Advance your slides on your Google slideshow without being Tethered to your desk I've been using it I used to have a [...] # Remote for Slides Chrome Extension
## Sara Hoffman
702 subscribers
14 likes

### Description
2461 views
Posted: 14 Feb 2023
Remote for Slides is a Chrome Extension that allows you to advance your Google Slide presentations with your cell phone. No more being tethered to your computer or your Smartboard/TV/Clevertouch! With Remote for Slides you can walk around and advance through your presentation using your phone. I used to have one of those remote clickers, but the battery in it was always dead then I lost the end piece to the clicker somewhere. 🤣 Also, when I used the clicker took up a usb slot on my Chomebook. [...] to go to a Google slideshow that you want to present so here's one that I have and once you have this Chrome extension added you will see in your slideshow you'll see present with remote we're used to seeing this slideshow start slideshow but this is going to pop up if you don't see it just refresh your page or shut your computer down and bring it back up so now I click present with remote and I'm in presentation mode and then over here you'll see there's a little arrow right here on the side and you're going to click on that and it's going to bring up this prompt remote for slides and you can click Start remote and then it gives you a six digit code that you will put into your phone you have to go to remote for slides.com on your phone so if you have Safari on your phone or Chrome on

Hello Mr. Smile,

There are add-ons that will allow you to control the Slides presentation from your phone. If you go to Extensions - Add-ons - Get add-ons, you can then search for Slides Remote Control. This will give you options that might work for you.

I hope this helps!
